code:
from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    if not triples:
        return ""
    # Assume all triples share the same subject (common for airport data)
    subject = triples[0].subject
    phrases: list[str] = []

    # Mapping of predicates to human‑readable phrase generators
    phrase_map = {
        # Airport‑specific predicates
        "cityServed": lambda o: f"serves the city of {o}",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevel": lambda o: f"is at an elevation of {o} metres above sea level",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevelInFeet": lambda o: f"is at an elevation of {o} feet above sea level",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevelInMetres": lambda o: f"is at an elevation of {o} metres above sea level",
        "location": lambda o: f"is located in {o}",
        "operatingOrganisation": lambda o: f"is operated by {o}",
        "runwayLength": lambda o: f"has a runway length of {o} metres",
        "runwayName": lambda o: f"has a runway named {o}",
        "country": lambda o: f"is in {o}",
        "isPartOf": lambda o: f"is part of {o}",
        # Runway details – handle numbered runways more naturally
        "1stRunwayLengthFeet": lambda o: f"its first runway is {o} feet long",
        "1stRunwaySurfaceType": lambda o: f"the surface of its first runway is {o}",
        "3rdRunwayLengthFeet": lambda o: f"its third runway is {o} feet long",
        "1stRunwayLengthMetre": lambda o: f"its first runway is {o} metres long",
        "4thRunwaySurfaceType": lambda o: f"the surface of its fourth runway is {o}",
        "5thRunwayNumber": lambda o: f"its fifth runway is numbered {o}",
        "largestCity": lambda o: f"its largest city is {o}",
        "4thRunwayLengthFeet": lambda o: f"its fourth runway is {o} feet long",
        "1stRunwayNumber": lambda o: f"its first runway is numbered {o}",
        "administrativeArrondissement": lambda o: f"is in the administrative arrondissement of {o}",
        "mayor": lambda o: f"its mayor is {o}",
        "2ndRunwaySurfaceType": lambda o: f"the surface of its second runway is {o}",
        "3rdRunwaySurfaceType": lambda o: f"the surface of its third runway is {o}",
        "runwaySurfaceType": lambda o: f"its runway surface is {o}",
        "officialLanguage": lambda o: f"its official language is {o}",
        "city": lambda o: f"is in the city of {o}",
        "jurisdiction": lambda o: f"is under the jurisdiction of {o}",
        "demonym": lambda o: f"its demonym is {o}",
        "aircraftHelicopter": lambda o: f"operates the helicopter {o}",
        "transportAircraft": lambda o: f"operates the transport aircraft {o}",
        "currency": lambda o: f"uses the currency {o}",
        "headquarter": lambda o: f"has its headquarters at {o}",
        "class": lambda o: f"belongs to the class {o}",
        "division": lambda o: f"belongs to the division {o}",
        "order": lambda o: f"belongs to the order {o}",
        "regionServed": lambda o: f"serves the region of {o}",
        "leaderTitle": lambda o: f"has the leader title {o}",
        "hubAirport": lambda o: f"has a hub airport at {o}",
        "aircraftFighter": lambda o: f"operates the fighter {o}",
        "attackAircraft": lambda o: f"operates the attack aircraft {o}",
        "battle": lambda o: f"was involved in the battle of {o}",
        "5thRunwaySurfaceType": lambda o: f"the surface of its fifth runway is {o}",
        "countySeat": lambda o: f"its county seat is {o}",
        "chief": lambda o: f"its chief is {o}",
        "foundedBy": lambda o: f"was founded by {o}",
        "postalCode": lambda o: f"has postal code {o}",
        "areaCode": lambda o: f"has area code {o}",
        "foundingYear": lambda o: f"was founded in {o}",
        "ceremonialCounty": lambda o: f"is in the ceremonial county of {o}",
        # Generic fall‑back – keep subject later
        "default": lambda s, p, o: f"{s} {p} {o}",
    }

    # Group triples by subject to avoid repeating the subject in each clause
    from collections import defaultdict
    subject_phrases: dict[str, list[str]] = defaultdict(list)

    for t in triples:
        generator = phrase_map.get(t.predicate)
        phrase = generator(t.object) if generator else f"{t.predicate} {t.object}"
        subject_phrases[t.subject].append(phrase)

    # Build a sentence for each distinct subject
    sentences: list[str] = []
    for subj, phrases in subject_phrases.items():
        if len(phrases) == 1:
            sentences.append(f"{subj} {phrases[0]}.")
        else:
            sentences.append(f"{subj} " + ", ".join(phrases[:-1]) + ", and " + phrases[-1] + ".")

    # Combine all subject sentences into the final output
    return " ".join(sentences)

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
